items.claim: Conor McGregor has a longer reach than Max Holloway.
items.quote: Conor's got a 74-in reach. Max Holloway, 69-in reach.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is naturally the heavier fighter compared to Max Holloway.
items.quote: It favors Conor early because he is the heavier guy naturally.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is not a grappler.
Conor McGregor has a history of performing well in high-pressure moments.
items.quote: Conor McGregor is a master of those big moments.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has been away from professional competition for five years.
items.quote: he's been away for 5 years now.

items.claim: Max Holloway has a higher volume of strikes landed than any other fighter in UFC history.
items.quote: He has no problem landing the most strikes in UFC history.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's cardio has historically been an issue in his fights.
items.quote: cardio. That's always been an issue for Conor McGregor.

items.claim: Max Holloway's chin is not necessarily compromised despite his recent knockout loss.
items.quote: I don't think that the chin is necessarily compromised of Max Holloway

items.claim: Conor McGregor's punching power is the last attribute to decline with age.
items.quote: the power is the last thing to go.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has never been effective in rounds two through five.
items.quote: he's never been good in rounds two, three, four, and five.
